title: MNRE Allocates Rs 44.4 Billion for Electrolyzer Manufacturing Under SIGHT II Program (with SIGHT I / SECI 1.5 GW Tranche-I context)
authors_or_org: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE), Government of India / Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI)
claim_supported: India's SIGHT II electrolyzer manufacturing incentive program allocates ~Rs 44.4 billion (~$535.6M) for 1,500 MW of competitive-bid manufacturing capacity (Bucket 1: 1,100 MW; Bucket 2A: 300 MW; Bucket 2B: 100 MW) for 2025-26 through 2029-30, base incentive ~Rs 4,440/kW year one (declining), requiring <=56 kWh/kg specific energy consumption and >=60,000-hour stack lifespan, plus 30-40% local value-addition minimums; sits under the broader National Green Hydrogen Mission (Rs 197.44B / ~$2.3B outlay through 2029-30, 5 Mtpa target). SIGHT-I Tranche 1 (Jan 2024) awarded 1.5 GW to Reliance, John Cockerill/Greenko, Jindal (300 MW each), plus Ohmium, Advait, and L&T Electrolysers.

Green-hydrogen (gH2) segment: sales EUR 459M, EBIT -EUR 56M, order intake only EUR 26M. Chlor-Alkali (CA) segment: sales EUR 386M (+double-digit growth), EBIT +EUR 58M, order intake EUR 322M. authors_or_org: Wood Mackenzie (case study); Sinopec (project owner, per PRNewswire release)

title: 碱性+PEM！电解槽采购花落谁家？(Alkaline + PEM: who won the electrolyzer procurement? -- China Energy Construction 125-set tender results)
authors_or_org: BJX News (北极星电力网/氢能网)
claim_supported: Chinese-language trade report on a China Energy Construction (中国能建) tender for 125 sets of electrolyzers (alkaline + PEM): LONGi Hydrogen, Peric Hydrogen, and Sungrow Hydrogen were the top three winners by value (RMB 7.26M, 7.18M, and 6.276M respectively); together these three suppliers have held ~70% of China's cumulative electrolyzer tender-win market share since 2023. China's total electrolyzer manufacturing capacity reached 47.7 GW/yr in 2024 and was projected to exceed 58 GW/yr in 2025, against roughly 2 GW/yr of actual global 2024 deployment (per IEA GHR 2025, L11-035) -- a large capacity/demand gap.
locator: Article body (Chinese-language, search-snippet synthesis; not directly fetched)
notes: Chinese-language primary-market trade reporting (local-language primary #2 of 2 Chinese sources) with concrete named-buyer tender-win data, directly cross-referenced against the IEA's capacity-glut figure -- important for red-teaming Chinese electrolyzer manufacturer over-capacity.

title: One of the largest green hydrogen projects in the world: thyssenkrupp signs contract to install over 2GW electrolysis plant for Air Products in NEOM
claim_supported: Air Products awarded thyssenkrupp Uhde Chlorine Engineers a contract for a >2 GW alkaline electrolysis plant (based on tk's 20 MW module) at the NEOM Green Hydrogen Company joint venture (NEOM/ACWA Power/Air Products) in Saudi Arabia; the plant is designed to produce 600 t/day of hydrogen feeding up to 1.2 Mt/yr of green ammonia for exclusive export by Air Products, backed by ~4 GW of combined solar/wind/storage renewable capacity, originally targeting 2026-2027 startup.
locator: Press release body (search-snippet synthesis; not directly fetched)
notes: One of the largest single electrolyzer equipment contracts identified this session; useful upper-bound reference point for gigawatt-scale electrolyzer procurement economics.

title: Ohmium secures 400 MW NTPC deal and 137 MW SECI SIGHT manufacturing contract; opens 2 GW Bengaluru gigafactory
authors_or_org: Ohmium International (press releases); pv magazine / iamrenew (trade coverage)
claim_supported: Ohmium's second India gigafactory (Doddaballapura, near Bengaluru, ~14,000 m2) launched July 2024 at 2 GW/yr PEM electrolyzer capacity (expandable to 4 GW), a Rs 2,000-crore facility; Ohmium separately secured a 400 MW deal with NTPC (2023), a Tata Projects strategic partnership (2024), and a SECI SIGHT contract to deploy a 137 MW electrolyzer manufacturing plant with an INR 202.76 crore (~USD 24.3M) incentive.

title: Japan picks first winners of $20bn hydrogen subsidy scheme for green steel and clean ammonia
authors_or_org: Fuel Cells Works (trade press, reporting METI CfD award)
claim_supported: Japan selected its first two projects under the JPY 3 trillion (~$20B) Contract-for-Difference hydrogen subsidy scheme: a 1,600 t/yr green hydrogen project led by Toyota Tsusho and a 20,000 t-NH3/yr domestic hydrogen/ammonia project led by Resonac, both targeting first supply in 2030.

title: Sungrow Hydrogen electrolyser orders: 45 MW China order and 48 MW China Coal Pingshuo coal-mine deployment
authors_or_org: QC Intel / gasworld (trade press, reporting Sungrow Hydrogen contracts)
claim_supported: Sungrow Hydrogen secured a 45 MW electrolyzer order in China (2026) and separately supplied 48 MW of alkaline electrolyzer equipment plus two purification systems (eight 1,200 Nm3/h units) for the first phase of a 600 MW solar-based renewable-hydrogen project supplying China Coal Pingshuo's open-pit coal mines in Shuozhou, Shanxi Province; Sungrow also confirmed alkaline/PEM shipments to Oman, Europe, and South America.
